trade.summary: South Korea is one of Israel's top Asian trading partners. Israel exports diamonds, pharmaceuticals, and electronic components; South Korea exports semiconductors, electronics, and vehicles. A free trade agreement has been in force since 2021, boosting bilateral commerce.

diplomatic.summary: Israel and South Korea share democratic values and have strong diplomatic ties since 1962. The FTA signed in 2021 elevated the relationship. South Korea has expressed solidarity with Israel but maintains careful neutrality on the Gaza conflict due to Arab market interests and UN dynamics.

military.summary: Significant defense cooperation. Israel has sold radar systems, UAVs, and other defense technology to South Korea. Both face regional adversaries and share intelligence on missile programs. South Korea has modeled aspects of its Iron Dome-equivalent programs on Israeli systems.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment, particularly in technology sectors. Korean conglomerates have invested in Israeli startups; Israeli firms seek Korean manufacturing partnerships. The FTA has expanded financial linkages.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. South Korea imports minimal Israeli energy products. Some cooperation on energy technology. South Korea is interested in Eastern Mediterranean LNG as a future option.

scientific.summary: Active S&T cooperation through bilateral agreement. Joint research in AI, biotech, and semiconductors. Israeli and Korean researchers collaborate across top universities. Growing trend of joint startup ventures.
